sections.paragraph:
(for אֲיֵמָה), f. terror, Deu 32:25. Followed by a genitive of the causer of terror to others. Pro 20:2, אֵימַת מֶלֶךְ “terror of a king,” which the royal majesty causes. Job 33:7, אֵימָתִי “my terror,” i.e. which I cause. With ה parag. אֵימָתָה Exo 15:16. Pl. אֵימֹות Psa 55:5.

Pl. אֵימִים-

(1) terrors, Psa 88:16.

(2) idols, Jer 50:38 so called from the terror which they cause to their worshippers. Comp. ·מִפְלֶצֶת

(3) Emim, pr.n. of a very ancient people, who are mentioned as having occupied the land of the Moabites before them, Gen 14:5 Deu 2:11.
